context: Tests plugin-specific skip-rate constants and gating logic for the real-time face+emotion pipeline. The exact values (face=3, emotion=10) are non-obvious tuning parameters from the plugin. Baseline agents guess wrong values or use time-based gating. General practices like downscaling, reading every frame, and JSON output are excluded.

checklist.name: Face recognition skip = EXACTLY 3
checklist.description: A named constant for face recognition skip interval is defined and its value is exactly 3 (e.g. FACE_RECOGNITION_EVERY = 3). Not 2, not 5 — exactly 3.

checklist.name: Emotion classification skip = EXACTLY 10
checklist.description: A named constant for emotion classification skip interval is defined and its value is exactly 10 (e.g. EMOTION_EVERY = 10). Not 5, not 15 — exactly 10.

checklist.name: Modulo counter gating (not time-based)
checklist.description: Both skip checks use `frame_count % CONSTANT == 0` (modulo of a frame counter), not a time-based check like time.time() or datetime. The modulo pattern is specified by the plugin.

checklist.name: Emotion guarded by face presence
checklist.description: Emotion classification is only called when a face crop or face detection result is available from the current or recent frame (guarded by a non-None / truthy check). The plugin specifies emotion runs only when faces are detected.

checklist.name: Different rates for face vs emotion
checklist.description: Face recognition and emotion classification fire at demonstrably different frequencies — emotion fires less often (every 10) than face recognition (every 3). Both constants must be present and distinct.
